title: Vidarbha: Brahmapuri records 47.2 degrees Celsius; red, orange alerts for some parts till May 25
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Maharashtra
full_text:
Vidarbha: Brahmapuri records 47.2 degrees Celsius; red, orange alerts for some parts till May 25
A severe heatwave gripped Maharashtra's Vidarbha region, with temperatures reaching 47.2 degrees Celsius in Brahmapuri and 47 degrees Celsius in Wardha on Friday.
Maharashtra's Vidarbha region reeled under severe heatwave conditions on Friday, led by Brahmapuri at 47.2 degrees Celsius and Wardha recording 47 degrees Celsius, with the weather department saying there was little possibility of respite for the next five days.
The weather department issued a 'red' alert for severe heat conditions in Akola, Amravati and Wardha for May 23, in Akola, Wardha, Amravati and Yavatmal on May 24.
It also issued an 'orange' alert for Nagpur, Yavatmal and Chandrapur on May 24 and for Akola, Amravati, Wardha and Yavatmal for May 25.
A 'red' alert signifies extreme heat conditions for the day, and severe heat wave persisting for two days, while an 'orange' alert signifies severe heat alert for the day and severe heat wave conditions persisting for two days, officials explained.
On Friday, Brahmapuri recorded 47.2 degrees Celsius, Wardha recorded 47 degrees Celsius, Amravati 46.6 degrees Celsius, Gadchiroli 46 degrees Celsius, Nagpur 45.7 degrees Celsius and Yavatmal 45.4 degrees Celsius, they added.
''Many parts of Vidarbha are reporting warm nights with above normal minimum temperature. This creates more discomfort to humans as well as sectors related to animal husbandry, fisheries, water resources, dairy and agriculture. Such conditions are likely to prevail for at least the next five days,'' Dr Praveen Kumar of India Meteorological Centre, Nagpur told PTI.
However, the 'Extended Range Forecast' issued by the Regional Meteorological Centre predicted the possibility of light rain and dip in maximum temperature from May 28.
(This story has not been edited by Devdiscourse staff and is auto-generated from a syndicated feed.)

title: Heatstroke Cases Rise As Power Consumption Hits Record 270.82 GW - Outlook India
full_text:
A relentless heatwave across vast swathes of India has pushed the country’s peak power demand to an unprecedented 270.82 gigawatts (GW) , as authorities scramble to manage a surge in heatstroke cases and strain on the electricity grid.
The record was set on May 21 at 3:45 PM, marking the fourth consecutive day of all-time highs.
Data from the Ministry of Power shows demand climbing sharply from 257.37 GW on May 18 to the current record, driven by the widespread use of air conditioners and coolers as temperatures soared past 45 degrees Celsius in multiple regions. On the same day, the national capital, New Delhi, recorded a scorching 45.3 degrees Celsius.
Vipul Kumar, Partner & Managing Director, Xynteo said: “India’s record peak power demand of 265 GW is a strong signal that the country’s power sector focus should shift from capacity addition to grid flexibility. Structural factors such as rising temperatures, rapid urbanisation, digital infrastructure, and household electricity consumption growth are making power demand concentrated and harder to predict.”
He added: “This requires investments in grid capacity additions, energy storage, and demand-side management. As India continues its economic growth journey, the ability to meet peak power demand reliably is critical to supporting the rapid electrification of the household and industrial sectors. We must shift focus from expanding infrastructure in isolation to creating an integrated, adaptive energy ecosystem that sustainably meets evolving consumption patterns.”
The extreme heat has been most severe in the northern and central belts, with the city of Banda in Uttar Pradesh hitting 47.6 degrees Celsius.
Health Emergency Across States
The brutal conditions are taking a heavy toll on public health. In Maharashtra alone, officials have reported 226 heatstroke cases and at least two confirmed deaths since March, with six other suspected fatalities under investigatio. In the West Vidarbha region, which includes Akola and Amravati, authorities have documented 131 heat-related cases, with two suspected deaths recently reported in Akola district.
Delhi recorded its first major severe heatstroke case of the season when a 24-year-old man was admitted to RML Hospital in an unconscious state. Doctors are treating the patient with emergency ice-water immersion protocols.

title: Maharashtra Weather Alert For May 23: Thunderstorms, Rain and Heatwave Warning Issued for Next 24 Hours; Yellow Alert in 7 Districts - PUNE PULSE
full_text:
Mumbai/Pune: Maharashtra is set to witness a major weather shift over the next 24 hours, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing a yellow alert for rain and thunderstorms in seven districts across the state. While several regions are likely to receive relief from scorching heat through pre-monsoon showers, Vidarbha continues to battle an intense heatwave with red and orange alerts in place.
According to the weather department, districts including Ratnagiri, Sindhudurg, Kolhapur, Satara, Solapur, Latur and Dharashiv are expected to receive light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning on May 23.
Konkan Region Likely to Receive Heavy Rainfall
In the Konkan belt, districts such as Ratnagiri and Sindhudurg are expected to witness cloudy skies and spells of heavy rain in isolated areas. Strong winds along coastal regions are also likely, prompting authorities to urge residents and fishermen to remain cautious.
The sudden change in weather after days of intense heat is expected to provide temporary relief to citizens and farmers.
Rain Alert Continues in Western Maharashtra
The IMD has also maintained a yellow alert for Kolhapur and Satara districts in Western Maharashtra. Thunderstorms accompanied by light to moderate rainfall are likely in ghat regions, while some areas may continue to experience cloudy weather and gusty winds.
In Solapur district, daytime temperatures are expected to remain high, but weather conditions may change by evening with chances of light rain and a slight dip in temperature.
Marathwada Districts Under Thunderstorm Warning
Latur and Dharashiv districts in Marathwada have also been placed under a thunderstorm alert. Weather officials have predicted lightning activity along with light to moderate rainfall in these regions.
Although no official alert has been issued for Beed, Parbhani and Nanded districts, isolated light showers are likely in some areas.
Vidarbha Faces Severe Heatwave Conditions
While several parts of Maharashtra may receive rain, Vidarbha continues to remain under severe heatwave conditions. Red alert warnings have been issued for Akola, Amravati and Wardha districts due to a sharp rise in temperatures.
Authorities have advised citizens to avoid stepping outdoors during afternoon hours, stay hydrated and take precautions against heatstroke.
Meanwhile, Chandrapur, Nagpur and Yavatmal districts are under orange alert for heatwave conditions, while Gadchiroli and Gondia districts have been placed under yellow alert.
Light Rain Expected in Pune, Nashik and Raigad
The weather department has also forecast isolated light rainfall in parts of Pune, Raigad, Nashik ghat areas and Ahilyanagar over the next 24 hours.
With rainfall activity in some regions and extreme heat in others, Maharashtra is currently witnessing a contrasting weather pattern across the state.

title: Maharashtra School Summer Vacation 2026: Full City-Wise Holiday List Amid Heatwave - newsd.in
full_text:
Maharashtra School Summer Vacation 2026: Maharashtra schools are still dealing with a hot and rough summer in 2026. Because temperatures are staying high in many places, many schools are keeping summer holidays going and some already changed class timings before the break.
According to a Maharashtra school holiday list for 2026-27, the main summer vacation is from 02 May 2026 to 18 June 2026, and schools are expected to reopen on 19 June 2026. Some schools may follow slightly different dates based on their own calendar and local rules.
Before the vacation started, several schools in the state shifted to early morning hours so children would not have to sit in the worst afternoon heat. One report noted that Maharashtra school timings were moved to around 7:00 am to 11:15 am during the heatwave period.
Schools Covered Across Maharashtra
The holiday pattern is being followed by many kinds of schools, including government schools, private schools, Army Public Schools, Air Force schools, and central schools such as Kendriya Vidyalayas. The main reason for the break is safety, since extreme heat can cause dehydration, tiredness, and heatstroke in children. Heatwave conditions across India in April and May 2026 pushed many states to change school hours or extend holidays, and Maharashtra was part of that wider response.
Here is the simple city-wise picture in Maharashtra.
- Mumbai: Schools are on summer break during May and June 2026. The city is hot and sticky, so holiday schedules are being followed to keep students safer.
- Pune: Schools are closed for summer vacation, and some had earlier changed school hours before the break because of rising temperatures.
- Nagpur: Schools are observing summer holidays because the city has seen very strong heatwave conditions.
- Nashik: Schools are following the summer holiday schedule, with some institutions like Podar Nashik listing 01 May 2026 to 03 June 2026 as summer vacation and 04 June 2026 as reopening for students.
- Thane: Schools are on annual summer break, and reopening can change if weather advisories say so.
- Aurangabad: Schools are closed for summer vacations because of the hot weather.
- Navi Mumbai: Schools are also on summer break in the Mumbai region, where heat and humidity stay high.
- Kolhapur: Schools are on vacation, and some local school calendars in Maharashtra include this area in the holiday schedule.
- Solapur: Schools are following the summer break while daytime heat remains strong.
- Amravati: Schools are on summer leave, with local authorities watching heat conditions in the Vidarbha belt.
- Akola: Schools are closed for summer vacation during the hottest part of the season.
- Chandrapur: Schools are also shut for summer holidays because temperatures are staying above normal.
Too much heat can make children sick very fast. Schools and education offices are trying to keep students safe by avoiding the hottest hours of the day and by keeping the vacation period in place until the weather becomes more manageable. Across Maharashtra, the same pattern is being seen in most government, private, Army, Air Force, and central schools.
